title: OpenELIS Analyzer Default Profile (canonical, v1.0)
description: Single source of truth for an analyzer's identity, transport, assay menu (analyzer_code <-> LOINC) and config defaults. One core + a protocol-discriminated branch (socket ASTM/HL7 vs FILE). Extra analyzer-specific keys are allowed; the required core is what consumers (OE2 AnalyzerService/AnalyzerRestController, bridge registry, mock profile_adapter, generate_analyzer_sql) parse. Confidence and format-consistency are separate axes: every profile MUST declare its data confidence, regardless of completeness.

$schema.description: Pointer to this canonical schema; uniform across all profiles.

confidence.description: Data-fidelity tier. Mapped from the Confluence Analyzer Integration Tracker (display scale uses MEDIUM-HIGH/N-A); tracker-absent analyzers fall back per the remediation plan. VALIDATED requires a real capture or real round-trip behind it (enforced by the CI gate).

category.description: Lab discipline. Constrained to the categories OE2 can section (generate_analyzer_sql CATEGORY_TO_SECTION_ID); immunoassay/POCT analyzers map to IMMUNOLOGY.

name.description: OE2 reads protocol.name/format. NOTE: mock templates use protocol.type — a deliberately separate key; do not collapse.

default_test_mappings.description: analyzer test_code -> LOINC. Required+non-empty for socket profiles; optional for FILE (column-map-only profiles like DT-Prime carry none).

test_code.description: The ONLY mapping key OE2 reads (AnalyzerServiceImpl). Renaming silently creates zero mappings.

loinc.description: Required non-empty; blank LOINC makes OE2 skip the mapping.
